jobs.title: Technical Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
About Trevor:

The Trevor Project is the leading suicide prevention and crisis intervention organization for LGBTQ+ young people. Trevor offers 24⁄7 crisis services, connecting highly trained counselors with LGBTQ+ young people whenever they need support. To drive prevention efforts, The Trevor Project also operates robust research, advocacy, education, and peer support programs. We’ve been saving lives every day for over 25 years.

Every day, and in every role, our staff contribute to our critical mission of ending suicide among lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer & questioning young people by embodying our values:

Heart–We care deeply and commit to do what matters

Integrity–We build trust through our words and actions

Community-Together, we are an unstoppable force for good

Belonging-We serve as allies to the mission, and to each other

Progress-We continually move onward and upward

Applicants and staff must be authorized to work for any employer in the United States.  The Trevor Project will not sponsor an employment visa for this position.

Role: Technical Project Manager

Location: This role will be remote in the continental United States, Alaska, or Hawaii

Reports to: Director of IT and Security

Classification: Exempt Full Time

Union Role?  No

Salary Range: $100,000 - $120,000

Summary:

This role will lead projects of key organizational significance, including global initiatives that support our operations in Mexico. Reporting directly to the Director of IT and Security, the Technical Project Manager will act as a bridge between all technical teams and organizational stakeholders to drive the successful implementation of enterprise-wide Technology initiatives. You will create comprehensive project plans, and manage cross-functional resources, interdependencies, timelines, and metrics of success for these projects to ensure that our projects are delivered on time, on budget, and meet the defined scope and acceptance. We are looking for an experienced Technical Project Manager who can bring technical rigor, strategic oversight, and a deep commitment to our mission.

Roles and Responsibilities:

Cultural Leadership

  • Contribute to our critical mission of ending suicide among lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer & questioning young people, while fostering an inclusive approach to your direct work and engagement with Trevor colleagues across the organization.

  • Demonstrate successful planning and problem-solving skills, including multitasking and working well within tight timelines.

  • Demonstrate attention to detail and accuracy, and bring fair, ethical, and equitable business practices to all work.

  • Learn eagerly, share knowledge appropriately, and improve continuously.

  • Other relevant duties and responsibilities as assigned.

Project & Strategic Leadership

  • Work with Technology and cross-departmental senior leadership and other program management leads to assess and define technology project roadmaps with clear deliverables and milestone dates.

  • Execute and revise project work plans as appropriate to meet changing needs and requirements.

  • Deliver projects on time ensuring quality, compliance, legal, and technical standards are met, exercising financial stewardship over project budgets of $500,000+.

  • Proactively identify, prioritize, and manage project risks, implementing mitigation strategies and escalating critical operational tasks when necessary.

  • Manage the administrative workflow of Requests for Proposals (RFPs) and Requests for Information (RFIs), ensuring vendors respond on time and all necessary documentation is collected for review.

Communication & Stakeholder Management

  • Communicate project success criteria results, metrics, test and deployment management activities, and other relevant information to stakeholders and leadership.

  • Create and maintain documentation including executive summaries, steering committee decks, weekly status reports, and sprint and project retrospectives.

  • Work, communicate, and collaborate effectively across all levels of the organization, utilizing strong conflict resolution skills to build consensus and keep projects aligned.

  • Partner with department leads to drive effective change management strategies, ensuring all staff are prepared for, trained on, and supported through technical transitions.

Advancing Our Mission Through Technology

  • Lead new project initiatives for performance management, new revenue models and offerings, improvements to our contact center.

  • Build new processes and procedures supported by software (MS Project, Jira, Asana, etc.) to streamline project onboarding.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Experience: 5+ years of dedicated professional experience in Technical Project Management, successfully leading complex, cross-functional projects that deliver technical solutions for diverse business units across an entire organization (from inception to deployment).

  • Organized: Exceptional attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple timelines and follow up on tasks without prompting.

  • Communication: Strong written and verbal communication skills, capable of effectively chasing down answers from vendors and internal stakeholders alike.

  • Tech Savviness: Advanced proficiency with project management tools (e.g., Jira, Monday.com, Smartsheet, Asana, MS Project) and office productivity suites.

  • Technical Fluency: Knowledge of software ecosystems, specifically understanding that applications require system integrations and hardware dependencies. You do not need to know how these function technically, but you must know they exist and be able to write clear tasks for technical teams to define the specifics.

  • Proficiency in spoken and written English

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Industry recognized certifications:

    • PMI - PMP Project Management Institute - Project Management Professional

    • PMI - CAPM Project Management Institute - Certified Associate in Project Management

    • PMI - CPMAI Project Management Institute - Certified Professional in Managing AI

    • PRINCE2

    • CSM Certified ScrumMaster

    • PMI-ACP Project Management Institute - Agile Certified Practitioner

    • SAFe Scaled Agile Framework

    • Six Sigma

  • Bilingual proficiency in English and Spanish (written and spoken)

Why Trevor?

Join a mission-driven organization where your work makes a real impact on the lives of LGBTQ+ young people—every single day.

jobs.jd_full:
Why should you join dLocal?
 
dLocal enables the biggest companies in the world to collect payments in 40 countries in emerging markets. Global brands rely on us to increase conversion rates and simplify payment expansion effortlessly. As both a payments processor and a merchant of record where we operate, we make it possible for our merchants to make inroads into the world’s fastest-growing, emerging markets. 
 
By joining us you will be a part of an amazing global team that makes it all happen. Being a part of dLocal means working with 1000+ teammates from 30+ different nationalities and developing an international career that impacts millions of people’s daily lives. We are builders, we never run from a challenge, we are customer-centric, and if this sounds like you, we know you will thrive in our team.
 
 
 


We are seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the vision, strategy, and execution of dLocal’s products. You will own the full product lifecycle, from discovery and definition to delivery and continuous improvement, working on solutions that drive value for both our customers and the business.
This role sits at the intersection of product innovation, business needs, and operational excellence, requiring close collaboration with engineering, data, design, and cross-functional teams.
You will be responsible for building products that meet customer needs, align with business objectives, and ensure scalability, reliability, and compliance where needed.
The ideal candidate should have proven experience in product management, preferably within financial services, payments, or other complex environments, and a strong track record of delivering scalable, secure, and impactful products.
What will I be doing?
Define and drive product vision, strategy, and roadmap, aligning with company goals and merchant needs. Lead the execution of the finance roadmap, including the integration of SAP for accounting and a Treasury Management System for treasury operations Develop and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders including customers, internal teams, and external partners Collaborate with global cross-functional teams, including engineering, design, and data science, to build and maintain innovative, scalable, and user-friendly financial systems Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify trends, opportunities, and threats to inform product strategy and roadmaps Lead migration initiatives to new balance and settlement systems, ensuring minimal merchant disruption and clear communication. Stay up to date on industry trends, regulatory changes, and new technologies shaping settlements in emerging markets.  

What skills do I need?
5+ years of product management experience in payments, fintech, or financial operations.  Experience in settlements, fund movements (cross-border, local, FX), treasury, or reconciliation is a plus.  Experience designing and delivering automated, scalable products. Strong analytical skills; comfortable working with large datasets to identify patterns, gaps, and opportunities. Excellent cross-functional collaboration skills, especially with engineering, treasury, finance, and compliance. Proven ability to prioritize and deliver high-impact product changes in fast-paced, high-growth environments. Strong communication skills, with the ability to translate complex financial concepts into clear requirements and merchant-facing documentation. Fluency in English is required; Spanish or Portuguese is a plus.  

What do we offer?
 
Besides the tailored benefits we have for each country, dLocal will help you thrive and go that extra mile by offering you:
- Flexibility: we have flexible schedules and we are driven by performance.
- Fintech industry: work in a dynamic and ever-evolving environment, with plenty to build and boost your creativity.
- Referral bonus program: our internal talents are the best recruiters - refer someone ideal for a role and get rewarded.
- Social budget: you'll get a monthly budget to chill out with your team (in person or remotely) and deepen your connections!
- dLocal Houses: want to rent a house to spend one week anywhere in the world coworking with your team? We’ve got your back!
 
Flexibility in how you work: We focus on impact and productivity over fixed hours. This means our teams have flexible schedules and, depending on your role and location, you will combine self‑managed focus time with moments of in‑person connection in our collaboration hubs.
 
What happens after you apply?
Our Talent Acquisition team is invested in creating the best candidate experience possible, so don’t worry, you will definitely hear from us. We will review your CV and keep you posted by email at every step of the process!
 
Also, you can check out our webpage, Linkedin and Youtube for more about dLocal!
